purpose: Canonical schema for the per-theme landscape JSONL that feeds Market_Map_Template.xlsx (rendered) and Market_Brief_Template.md (referenced). Every new theme's landscape file MUST match this schema. Skills (theme-first-pass, market-landscape, enrich-companies) read this file for schema reference only — they MUST strip records with any _template_* field before merging into a real theme.

record_kinds._theme_meta: First record in every theme's landscape JSONL. Carries theme-level fields that feed the Market Brief's narrative sections (the rendered xlsx contains only the company tables). Filled on first pass by theme-first-pass; updated as the thesis sharpens.
record_kinds.oss_project: Open-source project that defines or commoditizes the category. Not an acquisition target but in scope for landscape.
record_kinds.standard: Industry standard / consortium (e.g., OSI). Not a company.
record_kinds.end_user_buyer: Customer that publicly buys in this category. Signal source, not a target.

core_company_fields.name: Company name as it brands itself
core_company_fields.aliases: Array of alternate names, including dba's and rebrands
core_company_fields.buckets: Array of integer bucket IDs (theme-specific; document per theme)
core_company_fields.tags: Array of free-text tags surfacing notable attributes
core_company_fields.vintage_note: Free text: founding year + maturity signal
core_company_fields.total_funding_usd: Integer USD or null. CRITICAL: fill this from an authoritative single-record lookup (whatever firmographic provider is connected — Apollo, Grata, SourceScrub, Crunchbase, PitchBook — or time-aware web search); bulk/cached lookups silently return null on funding. See shared/research_rules.md Rule 5.
core_company_fields.source_note: Free text: where the name first surfaced
core_company_fields.added_in: String label for which sourcing pass added the record

provenance_pattern: Fact-bearing fields use nested objects: {status, _source, citation_url, citation_date, citation_snippet, confidence}. Top-level scalars get sibling *_citation fields. See shared/research_rules.md.

_template_example: _theme_meta — first record in every theme's landscape JSONL; feeds the Market Brief's narrative sections
definition: [Engineer-level definition, 1-2 sentences. Mechanically what the thing is — what's stored, what's computed, input/output. Define jargon inline.]
use_case: [Who buys this and what problem they solve. 1-2 sentences.]
strategic_value_for_firm: [Draft from Section 6 reasoning — synergy with your firm's portfolio + what's hard to commoditize. 1-2 sentences.]
incumbent_native_offering: [Feeds the Market Brief — one line on what a dominant platform vendor in this space ships natively that competes with the targets, OR 'N/A' if irrelevant.]
market_size_growth: [One line with a concrete number + source — e.g., '$X.XB market, YY% CAGR per Source YYYY']
key_trends_drivers: [2-3 bullets on what's changed in the last 12 months, comma-separated or stringified array]

_template_example: Outreach-worthy candidate — passes hard filters cleanly
name: Example Outreach Co
firm_fit_rationale: Filters pass cleanly (30 FTE, founded 2018, independent). [One-line on why this is outreach-worthy.]
_example_feature_flag_note: native_crm_connector and native_erp_connector below are EXAMPLE feature-flag fields — replace with the flags your shared/Screening_Criteria.md defines.

native_erp_connector.citation_snippet: Not found on integrations page; no marketplace listing

funding_citation.citation_snippet: Single-record firmographic enrich returned no funding events; bootstrapped per Tracxn

_template_example: Pass-OverFunded — category landmark, not an acquisition target
name: Example Landmark Co
firm_fit_rationale: $48M total raised (over $20M filter). 150 FTE. Category landmark — pure-play [category]. Not acquisition target.

funding_citation.citation_snippet: Single-record firmographic enrich returned $48M total funding across 3 rounds

_template_example: Pass-NotIndependent — product line of a parent entity
name: Example Product Brand
what_they_do: [Product description — but note this is a product brand of <Parent Co>]
vintage_note: Brand operates within parent company structure
source_note: Surfaced via [signal]; identity disambiguated against parent
firm_fit_rationale: Not an independently acquirable entity — product brand of <Parent>. Acquisition target would be the parent company, which needs its own hard-filter pass.
identity_disambiguation_note: Single-record firmographic enrich on the product-brand domain returned empty; parent company structure verified via website + LinkedIn.

_template_example: OSS project — not a company, but in scope for landscape
name: ExampleOSS
what_they_do: [Brief OSS positioning — what the project is and who maintains it]
source_note: Category standard; surfaces in every category survey

_template_example: End-user buyer (not a vendor) — useful as signal, not an acquisition target
name: Example Customer Co
what_they_do: [What the buyer does — not a vendor in this category]
source_note: Appears as a customer / buyer signal in category research
